title: Rustic Bookshelf with 2x2 Face Frames
summary: A fixed-shelf bookcase framed in visible 2x2 legs, with a planked back that doubles as the design's rustic texture.
description:
The 2x2 legs are structural and decorative at once — they're what the face-frame look is built around, and they're also strong enough that the shelves and top load straight into them rather than into thinner cabinet sides. Fixed shelves are the deliberate choice here rather than adjustable pins, specifically because a fixed shelf gives you something solid to nail the back planking to; an adjustable shelf would leave gaps behind it at every possible height.

A pocket-hole jig is genuinely optional on this build — the source gives a direct substitute (predrilled holes and 2-3/4" trim screws) for every joint a jig would otherwise handle, which is worth knowing before assuming this one requires the tool.

tools.note: Predrilled holes and 2-3/4" trim screws work as a substitute for every pocket-hole joint.

materials.name: Planking or 1x4s for back, 8 ft
materials.note: Tongue-and-groove boards or 1/4" panel also work.

materials.name: Trim screws, 2-3/4"
materials.note: Substitute if not using a pocket hole jig.

cutList.part: Top supports and bottom shelf trim

steps.title: Prep the sides
steps.body:
Drill 3/4" pocket holes along the side edges of the sides (add holes along the top edge too, for attaching the top later). Attach the side trim to the outsides.

If you don't have a pocket hole jig, carefully predrill and use 2-3/4" trim screws to attach the legs in the next step instead.

steps.title: Add the legs
steps.body: Attach the 2x2 legs to the sides.

steps.title: Build and attach the bottom shelf
steps.body: Build the bottom shelf first, the same way as the other shelves in the next step, then attach it to the bottom of the case.

steps.title: Build and insert the remaining shelves
steps.body: Build the remaining shelves and insert into the bookcase. Fixed shelves are recommended over adjustable ones here, since they give you something solid to attach the back planking to.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Build the top with 3/4" pocket holes and attach to the case. If you don't have a pocket hole jig, cut three 2x2s 11-1/4" long and screw between the top supports, then use these as an attachment point for the top boards instead.

steps.title: Add the back
steps.body:
Add the back planking, nailing to each fixed shelf. Tongue-and-groove boards or plain 1x4s both work as lower-cost substitutes.

Sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: Rustic bookshelf with visible 2x2 face frame legs
